search_results.title: How Brutalist Web Design Made a Comeback in Modern World - Zarma Type
search_results.description: # How Brutalist Web Design Made a Comeback in Modern World. After decades of having “clean and minimalistic” as the major trend, **brutalist web design** is slowly making a comeback. Its rough look, clashing colors, and minimum ornaments lend to more distinctive website designs. A perfect option to express creativity and unique ideas through your official or portfolio website. **Brutalist design** came to popularity in the UK during the 1950s and 1960s. **Brutalist web design** has elements similar to architecture in its design templates. The visual elements also look raw, and the color themes tend to clash. The visual elements of Brutalist design also make your website look distinctive. You can also express your personality with such a design. ## **Examples of Brutalist Web Design**. This design is the perfect combination of the stripped-bare style and the function-over-frills concept. With a custom font design, you can create the perfect Brutalist webpage that fits your taste and personality.

search_results.title: 8 Web Design Trends 2026 | VistaPrint US
search_results.description: # 8 top web design trends for 2026. That’s why staying up to date with web design trends 2026 is a smart move for small businesses. After speaking with our global community of freelance designers at 99designs by Vista, as well as our in-house design experts, we learned that web designers and business owners are utilizing more personal and human-centered web design. AI is in the mix, but instead of making web pages feel cold and machine-led, it’s sparking a push for designs that are warm, imperfect and full of character. Evolving from the 2025 web design trends, the theme that connects this year’s trends is a meaningful and human touch. In this guide, we’ll explain each web design trend and how you can incorporate it into your own business site to attract and connect with customers today. *Source: Web design combining the simplicity of brutalism with fun graphics by* *Aleksey Kostiuk* *via Dribbble*. *Source: Website design by* *weare.wildstudio* *via Dribbble*.

One can find this aesthetic in experimental digital art (for instance, the works of 3D artists like [**FVCKRENDER**](https://fvckrender.xyz/?utm_source=aigoodies.beehiiv.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=aesthetics-in-the-ai-era-visual-web-design-trends-for-2026), who creates opulent sci-fi landscapes with crystal and chrome elements) and in cutting-edge fashion visuals (e.g. Mugler’s tech couture presentations). search_results.title: 10 Website Design Trends for 2026 You Should Know
search_results.description: # 10 Website Design Trends for 2026 You Should Know. The shift from 2025 to 2026 marks a pivotal moment in web design. These trends represent solutions to real problems: designers and brands figuring out how to stand out when the baseline is higher, how to earn attention when it's scarcer, and how to signal craft and intention in their work. The following website design trends represent genuine opportunities for non-technical builders, with practical guidance on when each makes sense. This is a new interaction model in which AI creates a fully dynamic interface based on any user request, rather than simply generating text or content. Platforms like Lovable make this approachable through Agent Mode, which provides autonomous AI development with independent codebase exploration, proactive debugging, real-time web search, and automated problem-solving to build adaptive interfaces without requiring you to code the logic yourself. Test animations on mobile devices, and design with the ability for users to pause or disable motion for accessibility.

extracted_pages.content:
![Zarma Type Logo](https://zarmatype.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/zarmatype-logo-white.svg)

# How Brutalist Web Design Made a Comeback in Modern World

![7 0 unsplash](https://i0.wp.com/zarmatype.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/7-0-unsplash.jpg?fit=1200%2C5800&ssl=1)

After decades of having “clean and minimalistic” as the major trend, **brutalist web design** is slowly making a comeback. Its rough look, clashing colors, and [minimum ornaments](https://zarmatype.com/exploring-bold-minimalist-design/) lend to more distinctive website designs. A perfect option to express creativity and unique ideas through your official or portfolio website.

## **When Did The Brutalist Movement Start?**

![AD_4nXcawm1C 6DWrGYR dvtdzantAM YsO3pmWWW0OqVjWbc_dfRAqohWK7mOF eRCaNyWGqnwQCko2EzR41sxak78 ajfC9Ya_LmOv8yTM_GmzFSgY8SpjmGkZAa47kB6IU35rf4fRpH pxtRZy964O A?key=QrArzstllGS2NOMfXef BLbB](https://i0.wp.com/zarmatype.com/docsz/AD_4nXcawm1C-6DWrGYR-dvtdzantAM-YsO3pmWWW0OqVjWbc_dfRAqohWK7mOF-eRCaNyWGqnwQCko2EzR41sxak78-ajfC9Ya_LmOv8yTM_GmzFSgY8SpjmGkZAa47kB6IU35rf4fRpH-pxtRZy964O-A?fit=%2C5&ssl=1)

**Brutalist design** came to popularity in the UK during the 1950s and 1960s. The concept is rooted in the [resource limitations](http://architecture-history.org/schools/BRUTALISM.html) after the World War Two era despite rising demands for housing and other buildings. Brutalism answered those needs through bold, practical, no-frill architectural designs emphasizing functions over frills.

Many buildings with Brutalist style have monochrome palettes and bare structural elements. They often expose raw materials such as bricks, concrete layers, and plywood. Many notable buildings are known for their Brutalist approach, such as the Boston City Hall in Boston, Robarts Library in Toronto, and the Royal National Theatre in London.

The principles of **brutalist design** later translated into the digital world. In the mid-2010s, Brutalist web design started trending, especially among younger peopl

extracted_pages.title: 10 Website Design Trends for 2026 You Should Know
extracted_pages.content:
# 10 Website Design Trends for 2026 You Should Know

![10 Website Design Trends for 2026 You Should Know](https://lovable.dev/cdn-cgi/image/width=3840,f=auto,fit=scale-down,quality=95/content/guides/thumbnails/website-design-trends-2026.jpg)

The shift from 2025 to 2026 marks a pivotal moment in web design. After years of AI experimentation and template fatigue, designers are moving toward intentional, human-centered approaches that prioritize authenticity over algorithmic sameness. These trends represent solutions to real problems: designers and brands figuring out how to stand out when the baseline is higher, how to earn attention when it's scarcer, and how to signal craft and intention in their work.

Not every trend belongs on every site. The following website design trends represent genuine opportunities for non-technical builders, with practical guidance on when each makes sense.

## **1. AI-Powered Personalization: Websites That Adapt to Each Visitor**

Websites in 2026 evolve beyond static pages toward dynamic, personalized experiences. This is a new interaction model in which AI creates a fully dynamic interface based on any user request, rather than simply generating text or content.

### **How It Works**

AI analyzes visitor patterns (what they click, how long they stay, where they came from) and adjusts the interface in real time. A returning customer might see different hero content than a first-time visitor. Product recommendations surface based on browsing history rather than generic bestseller lists.

### **Key Considerations**

Personalization works best when you have enough traffic to generate meaningful patterns. Sites with limited traffic should start with simple segmentation, such as different messaging for different traffic sources, before building toward fully dynamic layouts.

Platforms like [Lovable](https://lovable.dev/) make this approachable through [Agent Mode](https://docs.lovable.dev/features/modes#agent-mode), which provides autonomou
